summary: The second-generation GR86 with the FA24 engine is proving reliable in early ownership. It addressed the oil consumption issues of the first-gen FA20 and has more power. Still too early for long-term data.

issues.name: Valve Spring Recall
issues.description: Toyota issued a stop-sale and recall for certain 2022 GR86s due to valve springs that could fracture and damage the engine. Verify recall completion.

issues.name: Throwout Bearing Noise
issues.description: Some manual transmission GR86s develop throwout bearing chirping when the clutch pedal is released. Annoying but typically not immediately harmful.

issues.name: Infotainment Screen Issues
issues.description: The 8-inch touchscreen can be slow to respond, occasionally freeze, or lose Apple CarPlay connection. Software updates improve but don't fully fix.

issues.name: Oil Temperature Concerns (Track Use)
issues.description: Oil temperatures can climb rapidly during sustained track use. The FA24 benefits from an oil cooler for frequent track day participants.

buyingTips: Verify the valve spring recall has been completed — this is the most critical item for 2022 models
The FA24 engine is a significant improvement over the first-gen FA20 — more power, better torque curve, and less prone to oil consumption
Listen for throwout bearing noise with the clutch pedal out — slight chirping is common but should not be severe
If you plan to track the car, budget for an oil cooler — the FA24 runs hot under sustained high-RPM driving
Check tire wear patterns — the GR86's rear-drive layout and enthusiast ownership means many are driven hard
